title: Founder Operating System

introduction.subtitle: Operating maturity assessment. 5 min.
introduction.description:
Founder Operating System helps you learn the vocabulary and typical expectations behind Focus & Trade-offs, Decision Cadence, Metrics & Evidence, Personal Sustainability. Use it as a private diagnostic: answer from observable evidence, not aspiration, and treat the result as a map for the next improvement cycle.

The score stays separate from the optional answer-support check, which asks whether your answers are based on opinion, anecdotes, documentation, or system-verified evidence. A high answer should mean the practice is visible, repeatable, and useful in real decisions.

This educational quiz runs entirely in your browser. You receive the baseline result without account creation or email.
introduction.duration: 8 diagnostic questions plus 4 evidence checks. Approximately 5 min.
introduction.privacyNote: Your answers stay in this browser. The result belongs to you. Sharing is explicit and share-safe.
introduction.disclaimer: Educational diagnostic only. Not legal, tax, financial, investment, medical, employment, regulatory, or psychometric certification advice.

questions.text: How many company-level priorities are truly active this quarter?

options.text: A. This is not explicit yet in Focus & Trade-offs; decisions depend on intuition, habit, or one-off judgement.
B. There is a plausible practice in Focus & Trade-offs, but evidence, ownership, or repeatability is still partial.
C. Focus & Trade-offs has a documented practice with examples, owners, and current evidence used in decisions.
D. Focus & Trade-offs is reviewed, measured over time, and strong enough to guide trade-offs under pressure.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Focus & Trade-offs: How many company-level priorities are truly active this quarter
conceptHelp.summary: Focus & Trade-offs means the discipline of choosing a small number of priorities and saying no to attractive work that dilutes the company. This question narrows the concept to "How many company-level priorities are truly active this quarter?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Focus & Trade-offs matters because founders, investors, partners, and teams need to know whether this area can guide decisions under pressure, not just sound good in a plan.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Use recent observable evidence: decisions, customer conversations, metrics, owners, records, reviews, policies, deal materials, or shipped work. Choose a high answer only when the evidence has changed a real choice.
conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would show how the team answers "How many company-level priorities are truly active this quarter?" today, who owns it, what evidence supports it, and one recent decision or trade-off it changed.

options.text: A. Authority is unclear, improvised, or concentrated in one person.
B. The practice is informal; boundaries, escalation, or records are inconsistent.
C. Owners, decision rules, and review points are documented for important cases.
D. Authority, evidence standards, exception handling, and periodic review are governed.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Decision Cadence: How are reversible decisions handled
conceptHelp.summary: Decision Cadence means the rhythm for making, recording, revisiting, and learning from decisions at the right speed for their risk. This question narrows the concept to "How are reversible decisions handled?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: Decision Cadence matters because founders, investors, partners, and teams need to know whether this area can guide decisions under pressure, not just sound good in a plan.

conceptHelp.example: A strong answer would show how the team answers "How are reversible decisions handled?" today, who owns it, what evidence supports it, and one recent decision or trade-off it changed.

questions.text: How are company goals connected to operating metrics?

options.text: A. The information is fragmented, missing, or too inconsistent to support decisions.
B. Partial tools or reports exist, but definitions and ownership are weak.
C. Definitions, owners, access rules, and regular review are governed.
D. Lineage, quality controls, exception handling, and a feedback loop into decisions exist.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Metrics & Evidence: How are company goals connected to operating metrics
conceptHelp.summary: Metrics & Evidence means using a few meaningful measures and observations to guide choices instead of drowning in vanity metrics or dashboards. This question narrows the concept to "How are company goals connected to operating metrics?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.

questions.text: How stable are your energy, attention, and recovery?

options.text: A. The area is mostly unmapped, reactive, or dependent on informal trust.
B. The area is known in broad terms, but controls, owners, or evidence are incomplete.
C. Owners, controls, thresholds, and response or remediation paths are documented.
D. Controls are tested, monitored, reviewed after incidents or changes, and integrated into operating governance.
Not sure / not applicable.

conceptHelp.title: Personal Sustainability: How stable are your energy, attention, and recovery
conceptHelp.summary: Personal Sustainability means the founder operating load: energy, attention, recovery, and whether the company can function without constant personal rescue. This question narrows the concept to "How stable are your energy, attention, and recovery?" so you can judge the real practice, not the ambition around it.

contextQuestions.text: What level of evidence supports your answers for Focus & Trade-offs?

options.text: Opinion: mostly judgement, memory, or individual perception.
Anecdotal: examples exist, but they are not systematically documented.
Documented: there are current notes, models, policies, metrics, or records.
System-verified: evidence is routinely produced by a governed workflow, system, or review cadence.

conceptHelp.title: Focus & Trade-offs
conceptHelp.summary: This question asks how well your answers about Focus & Trade-offs are supported. Focus & Trade-offs means the discipline of choosing a small number of priorities and saying no to attractive work that dilutes the company. The goal is learning: separate what you know from what you suspect, remember, or still need to verify.
conceptHelp.whyItMatters: A result about Focus & Trade-offs is more useful when you know whether it rests on opinion, a few examples, current documents, or system-verified evidence. Weak support does not make the answer useless; it tells you what to learn next.
conceptHelp.beforeAnswering: Choose the strongest support you actually have right now. Do not upgrade an answer because the practice feels important, and do not downgrade it if current records or systems really exist.
conceptHelp.example: If your Focus & Trade-offs answer is based on a recent metric, policy, deal document, customer record, or operating review, choose documented or system-verified. If it is mostly memory or judgement, choose opinion or anecdotal.

questionStageTitle: Learn the concept, then answer from evidence
questionStageDescription: Use the concept button when a term is unfamiliar. Prefer observable evidence over aspiration or ideal self-description.
contextDescription: This step is not scored. It records what your diagnostic answers are based on: opinion, anecdote, documentation, or system-verified evidence. Use it to decide how much to verify the result before acting on it.
